page: Amazon Web Services m8gb.xlarge is a general-purpose server instance powered by the AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. This instance features 4 dedicated physical cores and 4 vCPUs running at a clock speed of 2.8 GHz, with 1.0 thread per core. It is equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory, providing a balanced allocation of 4.0 GB of RAM per core. The CPU cache configuration includes 64 KB of L1d cache, 2 MB of L2 cache, and 36 MB of L3 cache. The instance operates on the Nitro hypervisor and does not include local storage or dedicated GPU hardware. Baseline network bandwidth is rated at 8 Gbps.

Benchmark data shows average performance across most standard workloads. The single-core and multi-core stress-ng tests yielded scores of 1707.4 and 6816.76 respectively, placing the instance in the middle 50% tier. Memory bandwidth is measured at 462.12 GB/sec for cached small blocks and 96.54 GB/sec for uncached large blocks. Single-threaded bzip3 decompression is a notable strength, scoring in the top 25% with 14.41 GB/sec. However, the instance exhibits weaknesses in cryptographic and extended instruction sets, with a PassMark encryption score of 753.68, placing it in the bottom 10%. Large language model inference speeds are also limited, with a 7B model text generation rate of 6.55 tokens per second.

From a cost-efficiency perspective, the ARM64 architecture delivers balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads without the overhead of hyperthreading. The lack of local storage requires reliance on network-attached storage, which is a key architectural tradeoff. Given the average performance in database operations and Redis SET operations, this instance is suited for static web serving, software compilation, text processing, and general-purpose application hosting.
description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.xlarge is a general-purpose server instance utilizing the ARM64 architecture with an AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor. It features 4 dedicated physical cores operating at 2.8 GHz, 16.0 GB of system memory, and a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps. The instance does not include local storage or a GPU, relying on the Nitro hypervisor. Performance benchmarks indicate average capabilities, with a stress-ng multi-core score of 6816.76 and a Redis SET throughput of 9.14 million operations per second. While single-threaded bzip3 decompression performs in the top 25%, cryptographic operations show relative weakness, scoring in the bottom 10% on PassMark. This instance offers balanced resource density for general-purpose workloads, making it suitable for static web serving, software compilation, and lightweight database operations.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose instance with 4 dedicated Graviton Neoverse-V2 cores, 16 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps network bandwidth, suited for web serving and software compilation.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8gb.xlarge is an ARM64 general-purpose server with 4 Graviton cores, 16 GB RAM, and 8 Gbps bandwidth for balanced workloads.
tagline: An ARM64 general-purpose instance featuring dedicated Graviton cores and balanced memory allocation for web serving and utility workloads.
bullet_points: Powered by a 4-core AWS Graviton ARMv9 Neoverse-V2 processor at 2.8 GHz
Equipped with 16.0 GB of system memory (4.0 GB per core)
Delivers 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth on the Nitro hypervisor
Strong single-threaded bzip3 decompression performance in the top 25%
Exhibits lower performance in cryptographic and extended instruction benchmarks
Suited for static web serving, software compilation, and general-purpose applications
